description:
## The arc, in one paragraph

fz today specializes function bodies per call-site Descrs (good), but the codegen
treats a missing-spec lookup as a `panic!` (bad). That conflates two distinct
questions: (A) "which compiled body do I dispatch to?" and (B) "does any clause
match this value?". BEAM doesn't have (A) Рђћ every fn has one body, dispatched
generically; (B) failures raise `FunctionClauseError` at runtime. fz already has
the BEAM analog of (B) (`:function_clause`). The fix is to give fz the BEAM
analog of (A): an **any-key spec for every reachable FnId**, used as the
**floor** that `SpecRegistry::resolve` falls back to when no narrower spec
covers. Narrowed specs stay as a pure optimization above the floor. The codegen
`panic!`s become unreachable.

## What stays
- `SpecRegistry` shape (FnId ├Ќ Descr-vec, two-tier resolve, minimal-subsumption).
- The reachability-driven specialization model Рђћ pure optimization above the floor.
- Block-env-narrowed codegen lookup.
- Cont spec keyspace Рђћ symmetric to direct fns, same fix.

## What changes
- **Policy**: every reachable FnId (and every reachable cont) gets an any-key spec.
- **`resolve` becomes total**: when subsumption finds nothing, return the any-key.
- **Pattern exhaustiveness respects guards**: a guarded clause doesn't claim
  coverage outside its guard. Standard ML/Erlang semantics.
- **Parser cond-context**: `if`/`while`/`case Рђд do`/`when` parse the condition
  in no-trailing-do-block mode, so `if pred(h) do Рђд end` parses as intended.

## What gets the boot
- `panic!("....29.11: no covering spec Рђд")` in `ir_codegen.rs:4005`.
- `panic!("....29.12.1: no covering spec for Cont Рђд")` in `ir_codegen.rs:3958`.
- The "exhaustiveness ignores guards" assumption in `pattern_check`.
- The unconditional trailing-`do ... end` sugar in expression-position cond.

## Compounding wins
- All HOFs (filter/map/fold + closures) stop hitting `.29.11`.
- Recursive return-type fixpoint gets a canonical anchor (the any-key spec).
- Pattern matrix (fz-ul4.43) compounds: guard-aware coverage diagnostics start matching user expectations.
- Macro DSLs (`when_then`, `loop`, custom block forms) unlock once cond-context parsing is right.
- A new `--Wspec-quality` diagnostic turns "did we specialize this?" into CI-checkable signal.

## Children + DAG

```mermaid
graph LR
    P[parser cond-context] --> Q
    E[guard-aware exhaustiveness] --> Q
    A[mandatory any-key registration] --> R[resolve falls back to any-key]
    R --> D[replace panics Рєњ degraded dispatch + quicksort fixture]
    D --> S[spec_check diagnostic]
    Q[quicksort fixture green]
```

`parser cond-context`, `guard-aware exhaustiveness`, and `mandatory any-key`
are independent leaves. `resolve fallback` needs `mandatory any-key`. The
keystone is `replace panics Рєњ degraded dispatch`, which converts soundness
from "panic" to "always-correct, sometimes-slow." `spec_check diagnostic` is
the optimization-quality follow-up; lands as P3.

## Open question parked
Bonus: do we recover *speed* by having the any-key body re-dispatch into
narrowed specs at runtime (PIC-style)? That's a separate ticket; not part of
this arc. The floor here is "any-key body has the full generic clause-test"
Рђћ soundness only. The ceiling is "any-key body type-tests then jumps into
the fast spec," following the `vr5b_typed_print` pattern.
title: any-key floor: fall-through-to-exception dispatch model
